calendar.matters:
PETITION Verified Petition Agaginst Attorney-In-Fact For: 1.Breach Of Powers Of Attorney 2. Imposition Of Constructive Trust 3. Accounting
Off Calendar Sep-16-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joseph Barton for Richard Fraige (Petitioner). Emily Knoles for Matthew Kessler (Respondent).

The Court noted that the civil action covers much of the same ground work but not the Power of Attorney issue, and then this action was filed. The Court proposed consolidating the probate action with the civil action (CGC-13-530485). The Court will prepare an order consolidating the matters. The Court will determine which case number all documents are to be filed in. Ms. Knoles stated that an action for an accounting was filed in the civil action, that no accounting was requested in the last 14 years, that all of the bank records, business records and rent records are in possession of Mr. Fraige, and that her Client does not have any access to any of the records. Ms. Knoles also stated that they would like to have access to all of the records to come up with a number which would help in mediation and that all records are in the property management company. Mr. Barton argued that the accounting issue should remain in the Probate Department, that all information was provided to his Client by Mr. Kessler and that Mr. Kessler should have all of the records. The Court suggested that the Parties agree on a Forensic Accountant to prepare one accounting, that the Parties share the costs and that the Parties think about how they will move forward on this litigation in the least combative way.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

PETITION Verified Petition Agaginst Attorney-In-Fact For: 1.Breach Of Powers Of Attorney 2. Imposition Of Constructive Trust 3. Accounting
Off Calendar Sep-09-2013 Continued To Sep-16-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
